questions.text: According to industry data, what percentage of Web3 jobs are filled through referrals and community reputation rather than public job postings?
questions.explanation: The 80/20 rule is widely cited: 80% of Web3 jobs are filled through referrals and community reputation. This makes networking the primary job search channel, not a supplement.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C

The 80/20 rule is widely cited: 80% of Web3 jobs are filled through referrals and community reputation. This makes networking the primary job search channel, not a supplement.

questions.text: What is the most effective FIRST action to build reputation in a protocol's Discord server?

options.text: DM the admin asking for a job
Post your project link in every channel
Consistently answer questions in the #help channel
Add a flashy animated profile picture

questions.explanation: Providing value by answering questions builds trust and visibility. Job requests and self-promotion before contribution are considered spam and damage reputation.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C

Providing value by answering questions builds trust and visibility. Job requests and self-promotion before contribution are considered spam and damage reputation.

questions.text: Which Twitter bio is most likely to attract a recruiter's attention?

options.text: “Blockchain enthusiast. Web3 believer. 🚀”
“Solidity Dev | Deployed staking pool on MST Testnet | Prev: ERC-721 marketplace | Open to freelance”
“Crypto trader. NFTs. DM for collab.”
“Student. Learning blockchain. Looking for opportunities.”

questions.explanation: A bio with specific skills, verifiable achievements (chain/contract), and a clear professional stance is 10x more effective than vague enthusiasm.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

A bio with specific skills, verifiable achievements (chain/contract), and a clear professional stance is 10x more effective than vague enthusiasm.

questions.text: You want to contribute to the MST ecosystem on GitHub. Which of the following is the best first contribution?

options.text: A major refactor of the SARAL protocol
Fixing a typo in the documentation and submitting a PR
Creating a new token standard without discussion
Forking the repository and never making a pull request

questions.explanation: Small, uncontroversial contributions (typo fixes, adding a test, improving a README) are the easiest way to get your first PR merged, build rapport with maintainers, and understand the contribution workflow.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Small, uncontroversial contributions (typo fixes, adding a test, improving a README) are the easiest way to get your first PR merged, build rapport with maintainers, and understand the contribution workflow.

questions.text: Why are hackathons considered “accelerated networking” events?

options.text: Because they provide free food and swag
Because 48 hours of intense collaboration builds relationships equivalent to months of online interaction, and judges/sponsors are often hiring managers
Because you can add “hackathon participant” to your CV
Because they are the only way to meet people in Web3

questions.explanation: The intensity, teamwork, and direct access to sponsors/judges who are often hiring create relationship depth that online chats cannot replicate. Winning or even participating opens doors.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

The intensity, teamwork, and direct access to sponsors/judges who are often hiring create relationship depth that online chats cannot replicate. Winning or even participating opens doors.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the MST Grant Program in the context of career building?

options.text: It is a charity that gives away free money
It funds your project, provides mentorship, and signals ecosystem credibility - making grant recipients heavily recruited by MST ecosystem startups
It replaces the need for a CV
It guarantees a job at MST Foundation

questions.explanation: Grants validate your technical ability and commitment. Recipients are seen as “ecosystem insiders” and are often approached by projects needing developers.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Grants validate your technical ability and commitment. Recipients are seen as “ecosystem insiders” and are often approached by projects needing developers.

questions.text: You have been active in the MST Discord for one month, answering questions daily. You see a job posting in the #jobs channel. What is the best way to express interest?

options.text: Immediately DM the hiring manager with your CV and salary expectations
Reply in the thread (if public) with a brief, professional message linking to your GitHub and a relevant contribution you made in the server
Post “interested” in the channel and wait
Ask a friend to refer you without mentioning your community activity

questions.explanation: Public, professional engagement that references your established community contributions is the most effective. It shows you understand the protocol and have already added value. Unsolicited DMs are still discouraged unless invited.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Public, professional engagement that references your established community contributions is the most effective. It shows you understand the protocol and have already added value. Unsolicited DMs are still discouraged unless invited.

questions.text: You have zero Web3 experience and a brand-new GitHub account. What is the most effective long-term strategy to build a network from scratch?

options.text: Pick one ecosystem (MST), learn its tech deeply, join its Discord/Forum, answer questions daily, build public projects, and document the journey on Twitter - for at least 6 months
Send your CV to 100 companies every week
Focus on getting as many Twitter followers as possible by following everyone
Wait until you are “ready” before engaging publicly

questions.explanation: Deep ecosystem focus, consistent public contribution, and documenting the learning journey build a reputation that attracts opportunities. Spray-and-pray CV sending and follower-chasing are ineffective.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: A

Deep ecosystem focus, consistent public contribution, and documenting the learning journey build a reputation that attracts opportunities. Spray-and-pray CV sending and follower-chasing are ineffective.

questions.text: Which of these actions is most likely to damage your Web3 reputation?

options.text: Writing a blog post with a minor technical inaccuracy
Sending unsolicited DMs to multiple community members asking for a job
Asking a question that was already answered in the FAQ
Tweeting less than once a week

questions.explanation: Unsolicited job-seeking DMs are the fastest way to be marked as spam and ignored. Honest mistakes or inactivity are far less damaging.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Unsolicited job-seeking DMs are the fastest way to be marked as spam and ignored. Honest mistakes or inactivity are far less damaging.

questions.text: A well-known MST core developer tweets: “Interesting challenge: how would you design a gas-efficient way to batch 100 token transfers?” You have a good idea. What should you do?

options.text: Reply with a concise technical thread explaining your approach (e.g., using a multi-call pattern), and include a link to a code snippet if possible
DM them: “Sir, I can solve this. Please give me a job.”
Like the tweet and hope they notice you
Post a generic reply: “Great question! Following.”

questions.explanation: A substantive technical reply demonstrates your expertise publicly, potentially getting seen by the core developer and their followers. Job requests are inappropriate; generic engagement is invisible.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: A

A substantive technical reply demonstrates your expertise publicly, potentially getting seen by the core developer and their followers. Job requests are inappropriate; generic engagement is invisible.
